title: Apple333 database phase
objective: Produce a reviewable, additive database change plan without connecting to, migrating, or modifying any database.

inScope: Document schema intent, ownership, compatibility, and rollback assumptions.
Validate Prisma schema syntax only with a non-secret inert connection value when required.
outOfScope: Migration execution, db push, reset, seed, drop, truncate, or data repair.

decisions.title: Plan before mutation
decisions.rationale: Database work requires ownership proof, migration review, backup evidence, compatibility analysis, and explicit release authority before execution.

steps.description: Record the intended model changes, affected entities, and known compatibility assumptions.
steps.expectedOutcome: A scoped migration design with no target database connection.

steps.description: Document migration ownership, backup, rollout, and rollback requirements for human review.
Run schema/client validation only where it performs no database mutation or connection.
steps.expectedOutcome: Static evidence that the plan is syntactically coherent.

nonGoals: Do not inspect production data or credentials.

commands.purpose: Validate Prisma schema syntax with a safe, non-production environment value.
Verify affected TypeScript contracts when source types change.

manualChecks: Review ownership, backup, compatibility, and rollback evidence before any later migration request.
Confirm the phase contains no database command that mutates data or schema.

deliverables.description: Captures database ownership, migration, compatibility, backup, and rollback review evidence.

risks.description: A migration can alter or make data unavailable when ownership, compatibility, or recovery evidence is incomplete.
risks.mitigation: Stop at planning until a separately approved release authorizes a reviewed migration on an isolated target.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: The phase creates a complete database change plan without changing any database.
The required production ownership, backup, and migration approvals are identified as future gates.

stopConditions: Stop if a request requires database connection, migration execution, or production schema inspection.
Stop if ownership or backup evidence is unavailable.
